scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched via the live devdocs.drift.com/llms.txt AI-agent index (100+ page docs tree) and each linked endpoint's own page. 5 read streams (the 4 pre-existing plus teams) and 11 write actions covering contact/account/message/conversation/timeline-event/GDPR lifecycle mutation. Excluded: SCIM provisioning (a separate identity-management sub-product with its own auth model), the App Admin API (app_uninstall/token_info — OAuth-app integration lifecycle, not customer data), playbooks/meetings (list-only detail resources with no create/update/delete counterpart and, for playbooks, no incremental value beyond what conversations/contacts already sync), custom-attribute-definition listing (schema introspection, not itself object data), and the emails/unsubscribe endpoint (a genuine dialect gap: its wire body is a bare JSON array of email strings, not an object, and this engine's write dialect only ever constructs an object body from record fields).

excluded.reason: single/multi-user detail by id; identical record shape to the users list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates an internal Drift agent/user's own profile (name/availability/role); a workspace-admin identity action, not a customer-data mutation this reverse-ETL surface targets
booked-meetings list scoped to the whole org; a scheduling/calendar-integration byproduct, not a core CRM-shaped object this pass prioritizes
single-account detail; identical record shape to the accounts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-conversation detail; identical record shape to the conversations list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

per-conversation message list requires a conversation-id fan-out with no natural top-level incremental stream shape; conversations themselves are already synced, and messages are reachable on demand rather than as a bulk warehouse-destined stream in this pass
formatted rendering of the same message data as the messages sub-resource; not a distinct record shape
returns raw attachment file bytes, not a JSON record
aggregate open/closed/pending conversation counts, not a syncable object stream
single-contact detail; identical record shape to the contacts stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
same contact-update mutation as update_contact, addressed by an alternate identifier (external/anonymous id) instead of the Drift contact id; not a distinct record shape
describes the organization's enabled custom contact field definitions (schema introspection), not contact object data itself

genuine dialect gap: the real wire body is a bare JSON array of email strings (not an object of record fields), and this engine's write dialect only ever builds an object body from a record's fields — no bare-array body shape is expressible today
bot playbook configuration list; automation/workflow config, not CRM-shaped customer data, and read-only with no create/update/delete counterpart to pair a write action against
conversational landing page configuration list; same automation/workflow-config scope narrowing as playbooks/list
per-user filtered view of the same team records the teams stream already reads in full for the org

uninstalls the entire OAuth app integration from the customer's Drift org, severing all API access; an integration-lifecycle action requiring client_id/client_secret query params, not a customer-data mutation
introspects OAuth token/scope/org metadata for the calling app; app-credential introspection, not syncable customer data
SCIM user provisioning search; a separate identity-management sub-product with its own SCIM bearer-token auth model, distinct from this connector's OAuth access_token scope
SCIM user provisioning create; separate identity-management auth model, out of scope for this connector
SCIM user provisioning update; separate identity-management auth model, out of scope for this connector
SCIM user deprovisioning; separate identity-management auth model, out of scope for this connector
